title: Practice time change
passage_text:
From: Coach Miller
To: Soccer Club
Subject: Practice time change

Hi,

Because the school field is being repaired, Friday’s soccer practice will be held at Green Park. Practice will start at 4:15 p.m., which is thirty minutes later than usual. Please bring your water bottle and wear running shoes because the ground at the park may be hard.

Best,
Coach Miller

宛先：Soccer Club
件名：練習時間の変更

それでは。
【和訳】Coach Miller

title: Art museum trip
passage_text:

To: Mia
Subject: Art museum trip

Hi,

I went to the city art museum yesterday with my aunt. There was a special exhibition about animals in paintings. My favorite painting showed a white horse running near the sea. The museum shop sold postcards of famous paintings, so I bought three for my friends.

Best,
Sofia

宛先：Mia
件名：美術館へのお出かけ

title: A City Garden
passage_text: There was an empty space beside the city hall for many years. Some neighbors wanted to make it useful, so they asked the city to let them make a garden. They planted vegetables and flowers there. During summer, children helped water the plants. The garden became a place where people could meet and talk.

title: Learning from Mistakes
passage_text: Maya joined a speech contest for the first time last year. She forgot some words during her speech and felt disappointed. After the contest, her teacher told her that mistakes could help her learn. Maya practiced speaking more slowly and using note cards. This year, she joined the contest again and spoke with confidence.

title: The Night Market
passage_text: In River City, a night market is held every Friday in summer. People can buy food, handmade bags, and small toys. Local musicians play music near the fountain, so many families enjoy eating dinner outside. The market also helps small shops because they can meet new customers.

title: Saving Water at School
passage_text: A junior high school started a project to save water. First, students checked how much water was used in the bathrooms and science rooms. Then, they made posters asking everyone to turn off taps carefully. After two months, the school used less water than before. The students were happy because their small actions made a difference.

title: A New Bus Route
passage_text: Many people in West Village used to walk twenty minutes to the nearest bus stop. This was difficult for elderly people, especially on rainy days. The town started a new bus route last month. The bus now stops in front of the community center six times a day. More people can visit the library, hospital, and shops easily.

passage_text: A hospital introduced a small robot to guide visitors. The hospital is large, and visitors often had trouble finding rooms. title: Rainwater for Plants

title: A Small Bakery
passage_text: A small bakery in Hill Town almost closed last year because fewer people were buying bread there. The owner, Mr. Clark, decided to try something new. He asked customers what kinds of bread they wanted and started selling bread made with local fruit. Soon, many people came to the bakery again. Mr. Clark now teaches baking classes on weekends, and students from the town often join them.

title: The Walking Bus
passage_text: At one elementary school, many parents were worried because the road near the school was busy. A teacher suggested a walking bus. Now, more students walk safely to school, and they get exercise every morning.

Their project helps children study and reduces waste.

title: Saving Water at School
passage_text: A junior high school started a project to save water. First, students checked how much water was used in the bathrooms and science rooms. Then, they made posters asking everyone to turn off taps carefully. After two months, the school used less water than before. The students were happy because their small actions made a difference.

title: A New Bus Route
passage_text: Many people in West Village used to walk twenty minutes to the nearest bus stop. This was difficult for elderly people, especially on rainy days. The town started a new bus route last month. The bus now stops in front of the community center six times a day. More people can visit the library, hospital, and shops easily.

title: Rainwater for Plants
passage_text: At Green Elementary School, students collect rainwater in large tanks. They use the water for flowers and vegetables in the school garden. title: A Small Bakery
passage_text: A small bakery in Hill Town almost closed last year because fewer people were buying bread there. The owner, Mr. Clark, decided to try something new. He asked customers what kinds of bread they wanted and started selling bread made with local fruit. Soon, many people came to the bakery again. Mr. Clark now teaches baking classes on weekends, and students from the town often join them.

title: The Walking Bus
passage_text: At one elementary school, many parents were worried because the road near the school was busy. A teacher suggested a walking bus. In a walking bus, adults walk with groups of children to school. The children meet at several points on the way, just like bus stops. Now, more students walk safely to school, and they get exercise every morning.

title: Old Computers
passage_text: Many people throw away computers when they buy new ones. However, some old computers can still be useful. A group of university students collects them, repairs them, and gives them to children who do not have computers at home. The students also teach the children how to use the Internet safely. Their project helps children study and reduces waste.
